fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Idaho Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Idaho - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity idaho, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to idaho, respiratory therapist compact idaho, idaho respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Idaho DOPL — Respiratory Therapy Licensure Board
board.parent_dept: Idaho Division of Occupational and Professional Licenses (DOPL)
board.mailing_address: 600 N. Curtis Road, Suite 200, Boise, ID 83706-1262

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Idaho.

steps.title: Register and Apply Online
steps.description: Create an account on the Idaho Board of Medicine's Applicant and Licensee Portal, complete the online application for an 'RT-Initial License', and pay the non-refundable $100 application fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official academic transcripts from your accredited respiratory therapy program to be sent directly from the educational institution to the Idaho Board of Medicine.

steps.title: Verify NBRC Certification
steps.description: Ensure the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) sends verification of your current Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT) certification directly to the Idaho Board of Medicine.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided after submitting your online application to complete the required fingerprint-based criminal background check through a Board-approved vendor.

steps.title: Provide Licensure Verification (for endorsement)
steps.description: If previously licensed in another state, arrange for verification of licensure from all states where you currently hold or have held a respiratory therapist license to be sent directly to the Idaho Board of Medicine.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Idaho respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Idaho have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Idaho. To work in Idaho, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Idaho?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Idaho while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Idaho
hero.dek: Respiratory Therapists seeking licensure in Idaho must apply online through the Idaho Board of Medicine's Applicant and Licensee Portal.

official_links.portal_name: Idaho Board of Medicine's Applicant and Licensee Portal
official_links.registration_steps: To create an account, visit the Applicant and Licensee Portal, click 'Register', and follow the prompts to provide personal details, create login credentials, and verify your email. Once registered, log in and select the 'RT-Initial License' application to begin.
